title: Intensely Crisp Kenyan Garlic Bake
description: A traditional Kenyan dish featuring roasted garlic, this recipe is a wood-fired bake that is both energizing and whole30-friendly, with a grassy and crisp texture.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the garlic cloves with olive oil, salt, black pepper, and ginger powder until they are evenly coated.
Spread the garlic cloves out in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the garlic in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until it is tender and lightly browned.
Remove the garlic from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley and lemon juice.
Return the garlic to the oven and roast for an additional 5 minutes, or until it is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the garlic from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the intensely crisp Kenyan garlic bake hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
